FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class RenderContext

Namespace
FluentGpu.Hooks
Assembly
FluentGpu.Hooks.dll

Per-component hook storage (ordered; rules-of-hooks = stable call order). In the signals-first model a component is a reactive computation (its render-effect); UseState<T>(T) is a Signal<T> whose only subscriber is that render-effect, so a setState re-renders ONLY this component's subtree (granular), and UseContext<T>(Context<T>) subscribes the render-effect to the provider's signal so a context change propagates to exactly the consumers that read it. High-frequency scalars get UseSignal<T>(T) bound straight to a node channel — no re-render at all.

public sealed class RenderContext

RequestRerender

Imperatively re-render THIS component (granular — schedules only this component's render-effect). Wired by the reconciler at mount. For escape-hatch callers (e.g. a Navigator's OnChange); state/context use signals.

public Action RequestRerender

Field Value

Action

PrefetchImage(string, int) 

public void PrefetchImage(string src, int decodePx)

Parameters

src string
decodePx int

RunAllCleanups()

Run every pending effect cleanup + dispose owned reactive primitives (component unmount).

public void RunAllCleanups()

UseAnimatedValue(float, float, Easing)

A value that eases toward target one step per render whenever the target changes. It steps ONLY when this component re-renders for some other reason — for motion prefer the retained engine path (UseSpring(AnimChannel, float, SpringParams, params object[])/UseTransition(AnimChannel, float, float, float, Easing, params object[])), which rides the compositor frame with no re-render.

public float UseAnimatedValue(float target, float durationMs = 180, Easing easing = Easing.EaseInOut)

Parameters

target float
durationMs float
easing Easing

Returns

float

UseComputed<T>(Func<T>)

A derived reactive value (the Solid createMemo): recomputes from the signals it reads, cached.

public Memo<T> UseComputed<T>(Func<T> compute)

Parameters

compute Func<T>

Returns

Memo<T>

Type Parameters

T

UseContext<T>(Context<T>)

Read the nearest provided value of context (or its default), subscribing this component to that provider's signal so a change re-renders exactly here (no prop drilling, no full-tree walk).

public T UseContext<T>(Context<T> context)

Parameters

context Context<T>

Returns

T

Type Parameters

T

keys Keyframe[]

min float

Parameters

effect Action
deps object[]

UseFloatSignal(float)

A persistent scalar signal for the hot path (slider/scroll/progress). Bind it to a node transform/paint channel (TransformBind/OpacityBind) for a value→pixels update that skips render/reconcile/layout.

public FloatSignal UseFloatSignal(float initial = 0)

Parameters

initial float

Returns

FloatSignal

UseImage(string, int, ImagePriority, string?)

Bind an async image and observe its load state (media-pipeline.md §5). Subscribes this component to the host's image-status epoch so a ready/failed transition re-renders just this component.

public ImageBinding UseImage(string src, int decodePx, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible, string? blurHash = null)

Parameters

src string
decodePx int
priority ImagePriority
blurHash string

Returns

ImageBinding

UseKeyframes(AnimChannel, Keyframe[], float, bool, params object[]) 

public void UseKeyframes(AnimChannel channel, Keyframe[] keys, float durationMs, bool loop = false, params object[] deps)

Parameters

channel AnimChannel
keys Keyframe[]
durationMs float
loop bool
deps object[]

UseLayoutEffect(Action, params object[])

Like UseEffect(Action, params object[]) but runs after layout, before paint (Bounds are valid). Phase 6.5.

public void UseLayoutEffect(Action effect, params object[] deps)

Parameters

effect Action
deps object[]

UseMemo<T>(Func<T>, params object[])

Memoize an expensive value; recomputes only when deps change (dep-array memo).

public T UseMemo<T>(Func<T> factory, params object[] deps)

factory Func<T>
deps object[]

UseReducer<TState, TAction>(Func<TState, TAction, TState>, TState)

State driven by a reducer (the React useReducer); dispatches fold over the latest committed state.

public (TState State, Action<TAction> Dispatch) UseReducer<TState, TAction>(Func<TState, TAction, TState> reducer, TState initial)

Parameters

reducer Func<TState, TAction, TState>
initial TState

Returns

(TState State, Action<TAction> Dispatch)

TState
TAction

UseRef<T>(T)

A stable mutable box that survives re-renders without triggering them.

public Ref<T> UseRef<T>(T initial)

Parameters

initial T

Returns

Ref<T>

Type Parameters

T

UseSignal<T>(T)

A persistent reactive cell you own (the Solid/Preact signal): read .Value in render to subscribe, or bind it to a node channel for a render-free update. Survives re-renders.

public Signal<T> UseSignal<T>(T initial)

Parameters

initial T

Returns

Signal<T>

Type Parameters

T

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
